summary: DHH recounts receiving a telemarketing call using the promise of eternal life through Jesus as a hook. He expresses disgust at the exploitation of religion for telemarketing purposes. After politely declining the first call, the same person called back a minute later with the identical pitch. He cut her off and hung up, concluding that telemarketing in Jesus's name should be forbidden.
key_points: Religious teachings are being cynically repurposed as telemarketing hooks
The caller's persistence—calling back a minute later with the same pitch—reveals the mechanical, impersonal nature of telemarketing
DHH sees this as a form of slander against Jesus, co-opting a religious figure for commercial motives
money_quotes: I doubt Jesus was ever interested in having his name used as a telemarketing hook.
There should be an 11th commandment: "Thou shall not telemarket (especially in my name)".
"You already did me", and hung up.
key_insight: Using religion as a telemarketing tactic is a crass exploitation that disrespects both the person being called and the faith being invoked.
